channel.title: peax - Splunkbase Apps Releases
channel.description: a list of Newly/updated Splunk apps

item.title: OTel YAML Validator - v1.0.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Minor updates for App Inspect.


Description:
OTel YAML Validator is an app designed to help you both create and validate OTel config files for Splunk Observability Cloud. Check this out if you are setting up OTel collectors or using SIM, RUM, APM, or metrics in Splunk O11y Cloud. The Create dashboard provides a point-and-click method of creating OTel config based on what you want to measure in your environment. The Validate dashboard provides code validation of the YAML against the public OTel spec, and shows a graph view of the receivers, processors, and exporters in your config. Combined with some useful links to provide examples - you'll have everything you need to get your O11y journey up and running.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Spavin

item.title: LCS Insights - v1.3.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Removed studio dashboard for compatibility with 9.x and fixed issue with missing lookup definition.


Short Description:
Sprint 3: Nine dashboards: Executive Summary (2), Hardware Strategy (3), Software Strategy (4)


Description:
This application was created by Cisco CX to help customers visualize technical debt and manage known risk. The initial publication has a single dashboard called Risk Overview which shows KPIs for Lifecycle risk, Vulnerability risk and Compliance risk. There will be 16 dashboards in total, released over the course of 5 MVPs.
item.dc:creator: Russell Byrne

item.title: Threat Hunting Essentials - v1.1.324 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Short Description:
Free Cyber Threat Hunting App to hunt APT, Threat Actors, Malware tools, exploits


Description:
Free Cyber Threat Hunting App to hunt APT, Threat Actors, Malware tools, exploits, etc. For Premium Version - Contact sales https://www.avertpoint.com/contactus.html It is a must-have app, which hunts for presence of advanced persistent threats (APTs), threat actors, state-sponsored attackers, malware, and exploits in your organization's assets. All detected malware, exploits, and threat actors are mapped to the MITRE ATT&CK framework, providing deep insight into potential risks. Threat Hunting Essentials detects a wide range of malware tools used by threat actors, including: 1. Backdoor. 2. Worms. 3. Ransomware. 4. Bots. 5. Trojan horses. 6. Keyloggers. 7. Rootkits. 8. Spyware. 9. Fileless malware. 10. Cryptojacking. 11. Wiper malware. 12. Adware. 14. Viruses 15. P2P-Worm 16. Internet Worms 17. Net-worm 18. Clickjacking 19. Cryptominer 20. Fileless malware You can easily configure the scan interval in the app’s settings. The real-time scan feature continuously monitors for threats in near real-time, as allowed by Splunk. Information Use: Your email address is used to improve detection logic based on your feedback. Additionally, we may notify you about app upgrades, updates, or changes to features, user agreements, or the privacy policy.
item.dc:creator: Cyber Essentials Avertpoint

item.title: Maps+ for Splunk - v4.6.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Maps+ for Splunk — Release Notes v4.6.2

Bug Fixes

Fixed: Markers not refreshing on auto-refresh panels

In Simple XML dashboards using a <refresh> interval, maps would appear to update but markers, tooltips, and popup values would stay frozen at the values from the initial page load. After multiple refresh cycles, duplicate markers could also appear on the map.

This release fixes the issue completely. Markers and tooltips now update correctly on every refresh cycle, and the marker count stays consistent with the underlying search results.

The fix also resolves a related JavaScript crash that would occur on the first auto-refresh when clustering was enabled, leaving the panel blank after the initial render.


Description:
The mapping equivalent of a Swiss Army knife for Splunk.
item.dc:creator: Splunk Works

item.title: REST storage/passwords Manager for Splunk - v2.1.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

What's New in 2.1.1

Improved CSV Import Experience

Drag-and-drop file upload

Clicking Import → Upload CSV now opens a modal with a drag-and-drop zone. Drag a CSV file directly from Finder or Explorer onto the zone, or click Browse… to use the OS file picker. Either path feeds into the same preview and validation flow.

Early file validation

Uploads are now validated before any parsing occurs:
- Files larger than 512 KB are rejected immediately with a clear error — no row-by-row processing
- CSVs missing the required username or password header columns are rejected with a single actionable message rather than generating a "username is required" error for every row in the file

Scrollable import results

The Import Complete modal now displays results in a scrollable container, so the outcome of every credential is visible regardless of how many rows were imported.

Capped preview errors

When a file has many invalid rows, the preview shows the first 10 validation errors followed by "…and N more rows skipped" rather than listing every skipped row.


Description:
An intuitive, full-featured, Javascript CRUD interface to the storage/passwords REST endpoint. If you're an app developer looking to securely store passwords for API's, Custom Alert Actions, Modular Inputs or to access any resource that requires a password, this is your tool! Create, update, delete, view passwords, change permissions, sharing and app scope. Ditch your curl commands and upgrade to this simple to use interface.
item.dc:creator: Splunk Works

item.title: Corelight App For Splunk - v2.5.9 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

New Features

  • Dashboards
    • Data Explorer
      • Asset Classification - Automated network asset classification and visualization

Improvements

  • Dashboards
    • Corelight Menu
      • External Corelight Resources - Links to "Corelight Youtube Channel" and "Corelight Threat Hunting Guide"
  • CIM Compliance
    • Improved mapping of corelight_known_* sourcetypes
    • Improved mapping of corelight_suricata_corelight to Intrusion Detection Data Model
    • Improved corelight_smtp sourcetype to extract src_user, src_user_domain
    • Improved corelight_files, corelight_files_red, and corelight_files_agg sourcetypes to mvappend md5, sha1, sha256 to file_hash field
    • Improved mapping of corelight_smb_files sourcetype to Data Access Data Model
    • Improved mapping of corelight_notice sourcetype to Alert Data Model
    • Improved mapping of corelight_http, corelight_http_red, and corelight_http_agg sourcetype to Web Application Data Model
  • Lookups
    • corelight_status_action
      • Mapped action field values to match standardized HTTP Response Status Codes for Web Data Model

Bug Fixes

  • Dashboards
    • Security Workflows
      • Alert Aggregations - fixed formatting of agg_id field to address scientific-notation value issue to enable dashboard drilldown
    • Data Explorer
      • Connections - fixed corelight_conn_agg sourcetype and applied tkn_conn_uid token throughout Parent Data Sources


Description:
The Corelight App for Splunk enables incident responders and threat hunters who use Splunk® and Splunk Enterprise Security to work faster and more effectively. The app and required TA extracts information and knowledge from Zeek (formerly known as Bro) via Corelight Sensors or open-source Zeek, resulting in powerful security insights through key traffic dashboards such as: Intel: Find IOCs from external sources matched in network traffic. Notices: See situations flagged by the Notice policy for further investigation. IP Interrogation: Identify anomalies by reviewing top protocol usage, internal vs. external connections, top connections by bytes transferred and more. Log Hunting: Accelerate your hunt by narrowing down many logs to only the logs that matter. Detections: Find and respond to off-port protocol usage, IOC matches, and other potentially interesting events. Connections: Gain situational awareness using lists of top services, ports, dataflows, originators, and responders. HTTP: Find suspicious HTTP transactions by reviewing a list of top host headers, originators, rare user agents and rare host headers. DNS: Detect DNS exfiltration by spotting queries to non-existent domains and high connection counts. Files: Find executables hidden in benign extensions and compressed files. Corelight Egress Monitor: Find risky North/South user connections to weak SSL versions.
item.dc:creator: Corelight Inc

item.title: Cyber Watch - v1.0.3 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Release notes for CyberWatch v1.0.2 Splunk app, covering the new AI Module (AI Chat, AI Incident Investigation, AI Correlation Optimizer pages), Home page improvements, Incident page enhancements, and bug fixes.

Version 1.0.3

🤖 New: AI Module (3 Pages)
A brand-new AI module has been added under the AI navigation section with three dedicated pages.

AI Chat (/ai)
A fully featured conversational AI interface for SOC analysts to query, investigate, and generate Splunk content.

Multi-thread support — Create and manage named conversation threads (saved to soc_ai index for persistence across sessions)
Thread management — Named thread creation modal with 50-character validation; threads can be archived or deleted
Multi-provider model selection — Supports OpenAI, Google Gemini, OpenRouter, and Ollama (local LLMs via proxy); model list dynamically fetched from the API
Model switcher — Right-side panel shows currently active model and allows switching models per session
Dashboard generation — Attachment menu allows generating Splunk Classic XML dashboards via an AI-driven wizard
Scroll-to-bottom button — Auto-enabled when the chat viewport is not at the bottom
Welcome state — Clean empty-state shown when no threads exist; prompts analyst to start a new thread
AI Incident Investigation (/ai_incident)
An AI-assisted investigation page that performs deep-dive analysis on specific security incidents.

Incident autocomplete — Live search/auto-suggest from the last 100 notables as you type an incident ID
Incident summary panel — Upon selecting an incident, a visual summary is displayed with:
Rule description, security domain, urgency, detected time
First response time and total response time (in minutes) calculated from history events
Timeline of all incident lifecycle events (status changes, owner updates, worknotes, AI investigations)
Configurable analysis depth:
Quick Analysis — Fast triage summary
Standard — Balanced threat analysis (default)
Deep Investigation — Comprehensive IOC and context mapping
Malware Investigation — Dedicated malware-focused analysis using the AIMalware module
Save Investigation — Completed AI analyses can be saved to the soc_ai index and referenced later
Past Investigations sidebar — Shows previous investigations for the same incident, paginated (5 per page), with re-load support
Multi-provider AI — Reads cyberwatch.conf → ai_settings and calls the active provider (OpenAI / Gemini / OpenRouter / Ollama)
AI Correlation Optimizer (/ai_correlation)
An AI-driven tool for analyzing and improving correlation search rules.

Live rule browser — Loads all active correlation searches from Splunk via REST API (/servicesNS/-/-/saved/searches), shows app name and severity badge per rule
Rule trigger stats — Displays alert count for the last 30 days alongside each rule
Analysis focus areas — Configurable checkboxes (loaded from ai_correlation.json) to target specific aspects: SPL optimization, coverage gaps, detection logic, MITRE mapping, etc.
Include notable events — Optional inclusion of 1–10 recent notable events as AI context
AI optimization output — Results rendered as a scored analysis with:
Summary and optimization score
Suggested improved SPL with Copy and Run in Splunk buttons
Coverage gap analysis and MITRE ATT&CK mapping recommendations
Save Analysis — Persist analysis results to Splunk for future reference
Recent Analysis history — Sidebar section shows past analyses for a selected rule
Collapsible sidebar sections — Active Rules, Analysis Settings, and Recent Analysis panels are independently collapsible
🏠 Home Page Improvements
Critical Notable counter — Header stat box now labeled "Critical Notable" (previously generic label) with real-time count and trend indicator
Percentage trend circles — Animated progress rings for Notable, Critical, Electricity, and Gas metrics with trend direction arrows
Optimized base searches — Switched to a single base search per time period that feeds all metric tiles, reducing redundant Splunk searches
Time period switching — Enhanced animation on 24h / 7d / 30d button transitions; previous search instances are properly revoked before creating new ones
Activity Center — Dedicated section displaying recent SOC activity
Correlation Events chart — Chart.js powered bar/line chart showing correlation event volume over the selected time period
Main chart filtering — Filter buttons toggle individual data series on the main trend chart
Real-time animated counters — Number metrics animate smoothly when values change between time periods
Header toast notifications — Status messages surfaced via toast banners within the header area
Trendline sparklines — Per-metric inline trendlines drawn using D3.js
🚨 Incident Page Improvements
Collapsible UI panels — Toggle buttons independently show/hide:
Timeline chart
Filter bar
Donut charts panel
AI Investigation integration — Each expanded incident row now includes an AI Investigate button that launches an in-context investigation via the
AIInvestigation
module, plus a Saved AI Investigations history section
Inline worknotes — Add and view worknotes directly within an expanded incident row without leaving the page; worknote history is fetched from Splunk in real time
Incident change history — Expanded rows display a full change history timeline (status changes, owner reassignments, worknotes) pulled from soc_summary and soc_notable
Search Results & Raw Event buttons — Direct links to Splunk search results and a raw event modal viewer within each expanded row
Status dropdown — Inline status change (New / In Progress / Pending / Closed) from the incident table, with history saved to Splunk
Owner assignment — "Assign to me" plus full Splunk user list dropdown directly from the incident table row
Donut chart sync with filters — Applying filters updates all five donut charts (Urgency, Status, Domain, Top Rules, Owner) in real time without re-running Splunk searches
Configurable pagination — Entries-per-page selector; pagination state maintained independently from filters
Animated loading state — Stacked-cards CSS loader shown while incident data is being fetched
Filter-to-chart live update — Clearing or applying filters instantly refreshes both the table and all visualization charts
🐛 Bug Fixes
Area Fix
AI Chat Fixed API key being lost when switching models — key is now re-fetched before saving model selection
AI Chat Thread creation no longer auto-fires on page load; welcome state shown when no threads exist
Incident Fixed incorrect status normalization (space-to-hyphen) breaking filter matching for "In Progress"
Incident Expand button click no longer propagates to the row click handler
Incident Donut charts now trigger a resize event when re-shown after being toggled off, fixing blank chart rendering
Home Duplicate search manager IDs eliminated; old managers are revoked before re-creating for a new time period
AI Correlation UI container now cleared before rebuild to prevent duplicate elements on re-initialization
AI Incident Analysis depth "malware" mode now routes to the dedicated AIMalware module, not the standard investigation flow
⚙️ Configuration & Setup
AI provider and model are configured centrally via Settings → AI and stored in cyberwatch.conf → [ai_settings] with keys: active_provider, active_model, active_key
API keys are stored securely in Splunk's credential store under realm cyberwatch_ai_<provider>
AI prompt templates are loaded from /static/app/cyberwatch/prompt/ (editable JSON/MD files)
All AI conversations and investigations are persisted to the soc_ai index with sourcetype ai_chat / ai_investigation


Description:
CyberWatch is a premium Security Operations Center (SOC) application for Splunk Enterprise that serves as a powerful alternative to Splunk Enterprise Security (ES). Built specifically for SOC environments, CyberWatch provides enterprise-grade security monitoring, incident management, and threat detection capabilities without the complexity and cost of Splunk ES. The app features an intuitive dark-themed dashboard that gives security teams real-time visibility into their security posture and threat landscape. Advanced incident management capabilities allow analysts to create, track, and resolve security incidents with full audit trails and team collaboration features. Integrated MITRE ATT&CK framework mapping provides visual threat intelligence and helps identify gaps in security controls and detection capabilities. CyberWatch's correlation engine enables security teams to build complex detection rules and automated workflows for threat hunting and incident response. The app features customizable security response playbooks and workflow automation to ensure consistent incident handling procedures across the organization. Built with modern web technologies and optimized for performance, CyberWatch supports distributed Splunk environments including search head clusters and indexer clusters. The app integrates seamlessly with existing Splunk security solutions and provides role-based access control for different user permissions. Perfect for organizations seeking enterprise security capabilities without Splunk ES licensing costs, CyberWatch transforms Splunk into a powerful SOC command center with premium security tools and automation capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Kumar Suman

item.title: Qualys Technology Add-on (TA) for Splunk - v1.11.22 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

-Parsing Enhancement for Host Detection


Description:
The Qualys Technology Add-on (TA) for Splunk is a Technology Add-On for Qualys Cloud Platform data. It fetches Vulnerability Management (VM), Web Application Scanning (WAS), Policy Compliance (PC), Container Security(CS), File Integrity Monitoring(FIM), Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR), Security Enterprise Mobility (SEM), Activity Log, KnowledgeBase (KB), Policy Compliance Reporting Services (PCRS), Cyber Security Asset Management(CSAM) and Certview data using modular input and indexes it which then can be searched using the Search app, Splunk Enterprise Security app or the Qualys VM App, WAS App or PC App, EDR App, FIM App, CS App , Policy Compliance Reporting Services (PCRS) App, Cyber Security Asset Management(CSAM) App , Certview App and TotalCloud for Splunk Enterprise.

item.title: Cato Networks CIM Add-on for Splunk - v1.0.2 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Cato Networks CIM Add-on for Splunk - Release Notes

Version 1.0.2 (EA)
- Initial release of the Cato Networks CIM Technology Add-on for Splunk
- Provides CIM-compliant field extractions and mappings for:
- Network Traffic
- Network Resolution (DNS)
- Web
- Intrusion Detection
- Authentication
- Malware
- Change (Account Management)


Short Description:
Technology add-on that maps Cato SASE platform events and flows to the Splunk Common Information Model (CIM).


Description:
Cato Networks provides a cloud-native, single-vendor SASE platform that converges networking and security in a global cloud service. The Cato Networks CIM Add-on for Splunk normalizes Cato platform events and flow telemetry to the Splunk Common Information Model (CIM). This add-on enables Cato telemetry to integrate seamlessly with CIM-based applications such as Splunk Enterprise Security. It provides field extractions, tags, and data model mappings for the following CIM data models: • Network Traffic • Intrusion Detection • Network Resolution (DNS) • Web • Authentication • Malware • Change (Account Management)
item.dc:creator: Cato Networks

item.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 - v6.0.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
The Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 allows a Splunk software administrator to pull service status, service messages, and management activity logs from the Office 365 Management API. You can collect: * Audit logs for Azure Active Directory, Sharepoint Online, and Exchange Online, supported by the Office 365 Management API. * Historical and current service status, and service messages for the corresponding Microsoft Office 365 Management API. * Data Loss Prevention on Microsoft Office 365 Management API. After the Splunk platform indexes the events, you can then directly analyze the data or use it as a contextual data feed to correlate with other data in the Splunk platform
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud - v5.0.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
The Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud: - Handles System Log event ingestion using Okta's REST API endpoints and simplifies data correlation. - Can periodically ingest Okta Universal Directory (UD) data, including users, groups, and apps. This data is not treated as time-series events. - Provides the inputs and CIM-compatible knowledge to use with other Splunk apps. The Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud provides complete data collection parity with the Okta Identity Cloud Add-on for Splunk, and corresponding features except the following two adaptive response actions: 1) Updating user's lifecycle/status, 2) Add or Remove a user from a group. In addition to this, Splunk built TA provides comprehensive CIM coverage, high reliability and multiple features and enhancements. Please see the Reference section of the documentation for more details. The Splunk Add-on for Okta Identity Cloud provides support of the below-mentioned sourcetypes: OktaIM2:log OktaIM2:user OktaIM2:group OktaIM2:app OktaIM2:groupUser OktaIM2:appUser
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Request Builder - v1.0.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

1.0.0 - Initial Release

Features
- Custom | req streaming search command for outbound HTTP/HTTPS requests directly from SPL
- Supports all HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
- Custom headers, request body, cookies, and timeout control
- Secure credential management via Splunk Storage Passwords (Basic Auth, Bearer Token, API Key)
- SSL/TLS verification with configurable toggle per request
- Events enriched with response status code, body, and headers
- Comprehensive search assistant with full syntax documentation and examples

Authentication
- basic — HTTP Basic Auth
- bearer — Bearer token via Authorization header
- apikey — API key via custom header (default: x-api-key)

Included
- Interactive home dashboard with quick start examples
- Full README with installation, configuration, and troubleshooting guide


Short Description:
Make outbound HTTP/HTTPS API calls directly from SPL. The | req command enriches events with API responses, triggers webhooks, and integrates Splunk with any REST endpoint — supporting Basic, Bearer, and API key auth via Splunk Storage Passwords.


Description:
Request Builder adds the | req custom SPL command, letting you make outbound HTTP and HTTPS requests directly from the Splunk search pipeline without any extra middleware. The problem it solves: Splunk has no built-in way to call external REST APIs or webhooks during a search. Request Builder fills that gap by turning every event into a potential HTTP request — you can enrich events with live API responses, trigger SOAR or CI/CD workflows from alert actions, post data to ticketing or notification systems, or pull inventory and configuration from any REST endpoint, all with standard SPL syntax. Key capabilities: - Supports GET, POST methods - Custom request headers and JSON or plain-text request bodies - Cookie injection and configurable SSL verification (with certifi CA bundle) - Per-request timeout control - Authentication via HTTP Basic Auth, ****** or API key header — credentials stored securely in Splunk Storage Passwords, never in plain text in searches - Adds status_code, response, response_headers, and ssl_verify fields to each enriched event - Works across Splunk Enterprise 8.x+ and Splunk Cloud
item.dc:creator: elif kaplans

item.title: Hitachi VSP One Block Add-on for Splunk - v1.0.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Version 1.0.0

Release Date: March 20, 2026

Initial Release

Data Collection

  • Three modular inputs for VSP 360 block storage data collection: Configuration, Performance, and Synthetic
  • OAuth2 client-credentials authentication with automatic token refresh
  • HTTPS-only enforcement with SSL certificate verification
  • Configurable resource type filtering (collect_all_resource_types or resource_types_csv)
  • Attribute chunking for large API queries (max_attributes_per_request)
  • Configurable UTC offset for global deployments

Sourcetypes

  • hitachi:vspblock:config — scalar configuration attributes (one event per attribute per instance)
  • hitachi:vspblock:perf — timeseries performance metrics (one event per sample point)
  • hitachi:vspblock:synth — synthetic roll-up timeseries metrics (one event per sample point)

Supported Resource Types

  • raidStorage, raidPool, raidLdev, raidPort, raidPG, raidClpr, raidMPB, and additional types defined in the embedded schema

Reliability

  • Per-resource-type checkpointing with automatic resume on restart
  • 60-second duplicate guard against rapid scheduler triggers
  • Safe checkpoint behavior: no advancement when zero events are returned
  • Null-filling of missing schema attributes for consistent field coverage
  • Storage identity enrichment (name, model) injected into every performance and synthetic event

Compatibility

  • Splunk Enterprise 10.x
  • Splunk Cloud
  • Hitachi VSP One Block Storage Arrays

Known Limitations

  • Resource types not registered in the embedded schema (vsp360_schema.py) are skipped with a warning
  • The add-on requires direct HTTPS connectivity to VSP 360 on port 443


Short Description:
Collects configuration, performance, and synthetic metrics from Hitachi VSP One Block storage arrays via the VSP 360 REST API.


Description:
This add-on connects Splunk to Hitachi VSP block storage arrays through the VSP 360 REST API. It collects configuration, performance, and synthetic metrics exposed by VSP 360 using OAuth2 client‑credentials authentication and indexes the data as structured JSON events for search, alerting, and visualization. It brings VSP 360 telemetry into Splunk to enable centralized monitoring, fleet‑wide analytics, and correlation with other infrastructure data. The add-on includes three modular inputs that automatically collect and normalize VSP 360 data into Splunk. What it collects: Configuration metrics: Storage system identity, pool capacity, LDEV attributes, port details, parity group layout, CLPR settings (sourcetype: hitachi:vspblock:config) Performance metrics: IOPS, response time, transfer rate, cache utilization, write pending, processor busy, and port throughput as timeseries data (sourcetype: hitachi:vspblock:perf) Synthetic metrics: Roll-up and derived timeseries metrics computed by VSP 360 (sourcetype: hitachi:vspblock:synth)
item.dc:creator: Karthik Raja Balasubramanian

item.title: Hitachi VSP One Block App for Splunk - v1.0.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Version 1.0.0

Release Date: March 20, 2026

Initial Release

Dashboards

  • Fleet Operations & Performance — fleet-level KPIs, pool capacity trends, array IOPS/transfer rate, cache utilization, write pending by CLPR, MPB processor utilization, port performance
  • LDEV, Pool & PG Performance Analytics — Top 10 LDEV analysis (IOPS, response time, transfer rate, random/sequential breakdowns), pool performance, parity group utilization and performance

Features

  • Built with Splunk Dashboard Studio (JSON-based)
  • Three global dropdown filters: VSP 360 Host, Storage Serial Number, Time Range
  • 45 visualizations across both dashboards
  • Optimized SPL queries with timechart, eventstats, and fillnull for smooth trend lines

Compatibility

  • Splunk Enterprise 10.x
  • Splunk Cloud
  • Requires VSP One Block Add-on via VSP 360 for data collection

Known Limitations

  • Dashboards use hitachicsad as the default index; manual edit required if using a different index name
  • Some panels may appear empty if the corresponding resource types are not collected by the add-on


Short Description:
Provides two Dashboard Studio dashboards for monitoring Hitachi VSP One Block storage arrays using data collected by the companion VSP One Block Add-on via VSP 360.


Description:
This Splunk app provides two prebuilt Dashboard Studio dashboards for monitoring and analyzing Hitachi VSP One Block storage arrays. It visualizes configuration and performance data collected by the companion VSP One Block Add-on via the VSP 360 REST API. It helps administrators monitor fleet-level storage KPIs at a glance, track pool capacity trends and subscription rates over time, analyze array-level IOPS, transfer rates, and cache utilization, identify top LDEV performers by IOPS, response time, and transfer rate, monitor pool and parity group performance trends, and correlate port throughput and processor utilization across arrays. Dashboards included: Fleet Operations & Performance: Storage system KPI tiles (capacity, used, free, allocated TiB, data reduction ratio, volume and port counts), pool capacity trend lines (Used vs Subscribed TiB, Used% vs Subscription Rate%), array-level IOPS and transfer rate, cache utilization and write pending by CLPR, MPB processor utilization, and port performance (IOPS, response time, utilization, transfer rate). LDEV, Pool & PG Performance Analytics: Top 10 LDEV IOPS (total, read, write), Top 10 LDEV response time (total, read, write), Top 10 LDEV transfer rate (total, read, write), Top 10 LDEV random IOPS and transfer rate (read, write), Top 10 LDEV sequential IOPS and transfer rate (read, write), pool IOPS, response time, and transfer rate, and parity group utilization, IOPS, transfer rate, and response time. Data source: This app requires the VSP One Block Add-on via VSP 360 to collect data. The add-on indexes events into Splunk using three sourcetypes: hitachi:vspblock:config (configuration attributes), hitachi:vspblock:perf (performance timeseries), and hitachi:vspblock:synth (synthetic roll-up metrics). All dashboards query the hitachicsad index by default.
item.dc:creator: Karthik Raja Balasubramanian

item.title: Event builder For Splunk - v2.0.1 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Event Builder For Splunk v2.0.1

Initial public release.

This release introduces the Event Builder For Splunk companion app for Splunkbase, designed to work alongside the Event Builder For Splunk desktop application (v2.0.1) for security data simulation and SOC lab environments.

What's included

  • 21 purpose-built security indexes with 90-day data retention:
    web, network, linux, windows, dns, vpn, aws, gcp, ids, email, o365, database, proxy, kubernetes, edr, identity, virtualization, saas, monitoring, itsm, pam
  • Field extraction rules for 42 sourcetypes covering endpoint, network, cloud, identity, EDR, PAM, and infrastructure log formats
  • 8 dashboards:
  • Landing
  • Overview (30s auto-refresh)
  • Live Monitor (real-time rt search, 10s refresh)
  • Identity & Access
  • Network & Infrastructure
  • Endpoint & Cloud
  • Threat Detections
  • Setup Guide
  • 10 pre-built correlation detection rules (all disabled by default):
  • Brute Force
  • Impossible Travel
  • MFA Bypass
  • Kubernetes Privileged Container
  • AWS IAM Escalation
  • Pass-the-Hash
  • C2 Beaconing
  • Data Exfiltration
  • Credential Dumping
  • PAM Outside Hours
  • 4 SPL macros for cross-domain search:
  • eb_all_indexes
  • eb_security_indexes
  • eb_network_indexes
  • eb_cloud_indexes
  • 3 lookup tables for severity mapping, index domain classification, and sourcetype labeling
  • Compatible with Splunk Enterprise 9.x
  • AppInspect compliant


Short Description:
Generate realistic synthetic security log events across 42 sourcetypes and 21 indexes. Ideal for SOC training, detection engineering, and dashboard testing without production data.


Description:
Event Builder For Splunk is a security data simulation platform that generates realistic synthetic log events across 42 sourcetypes and 21 security data domains. It enables SOC analysts, detection engineers, and security trainers to populate Splunk indexes with realistic data for dashboard testing, detection rule validation, and threat hunting exercises — without requiring access to a live production environment. The companion desktop application delivers events directly to Splunk via HTTP Event Collector (HEC) in real time.
item.dc:creator: sevion xia

item.title: AI Query Assistant for Splunk - v2.2.7 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Short Description:
Use natural language to generate SPL queries in Splunk with support for multiple AI providers, query history, templates, and enterprise security guardrails.


Description:
AI Query Assistant for Splunk is an AI-powered app that enables users to query and analyze Splunk data using natural language. It helps convert plain-language questions into structured SPL searches, reducing the learning curve for SPL and improving analyst efficiency. The app is designed for security operations, IT operations, and data exploration scenarios where users need faster and more intuitive access to Splunk data. It supports multiple AI providers, including OpenAI-compatible APIs and Anthropic Claude, and provides configurable safeguards to help reduce the risk of unsafe or invalid query generation. Key capabilities include natural language to SPL conversion, AI provider management, query history, reusable query templates, and controlled enterprise usage with license-based activation. This app is intended for organizations that want to improve Splunk usability while maintaining operational control and security.
item.dc:creator: sevion xia

item.title: Splunk AI Assistant for SPL - v1.5.1 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
Splunk AI Assistant for SPL offers bi-directional translation between natural language (NL) and Splunk Search Processing Language (SPL) with the ability to be fully personalized in context of your unique environment and data. What Splunk’s AI Assistant for SPL can do for you: With Agentic Response Generation, all capabilities are seamlessly integrated into a single chat window. The assistant now processes your requests with advanced reasoning and tool-calling, providing: 📝 Intelligent SPL Generation: Compose what you want to search in plain English, and the assistant translates the request into Splunk Search Processing Language (SPL). This feature helps you write valid, accurate queries, even for complex prompts, providing a strong baseline for your data analysis. 🚀 SPL Optimization: Now generally available, the assistant can optimize your existing and newly generated searches to execute faster and consume fewer resources, leading to quicker insights and more efficient operations. 📖 SPL Explanation: Understand any SPL search in plain English, complete with a detailed breakdown of its logic and purpose. 📚 Knowledge Retrieval: Get answers to questions about Splunk documentation, platform terms, or products. The assistant exposes its reasoning steps to build trust and asks clarifying questions for ambiguous prompts, ensuring more accurate and reliable outputs. Personalization Personalization in Splunk AI Assistant for SPL is an optional feature that generates more accurate and contextual SPL results by considering your unique data, environment, and past queries, while only using non-personal data and supporting role-based access controls. This helps users build better, more relevant SPL searches and distinguishes Splunk’s AI Assistant from generic third-party tools. More Model Runtime Choices (General Availability) Splunk AI Assistant for SPL now leverages models in Azure OpenAI in addition to Splunk-hosted Large Language Models (LLMs) for even better response quality, expanding the range of powerful LLMs at its disposal. (c) Splunk 2026. All rights reserved.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Splunk MCP Server - v1.0.5 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Fixed: RSA key pair conflicts on Search Head Clusters due to replication lag.


Description:
MCP Server for Splunk Platform The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard and framework that enables seamless, secure, and standardized two-way communication between AI applications (like large language models) and external data sources or tools. It acts as a universal adapter allowing AI systems to access, execute, and integrate functionalities from diverse systems through a common protocol, simplifying data sharing and tool interoperability without custom coding for each integration. Splunk's Model Context Protocol (MCP) server leverages this to provide a standardized, secure, and scalable interface to connect AI assistants, agents, and other intelligent systems with data in the Splunk platform for both Enterprise & Cloud customers in beta. 🔑 Key Features - Universal Connectivity Seamlessly connects AI agents and tools to Splunk data resources in a secure and efficient manner. - Enterprise-Grade Security Includes built-in authentication, authorization, and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC). - Rapid Deployment Offers a plug-and-play solution, eliminating the need for custom integrations. ⚙️ Core Capabilities - Explore the Data Navigate and interact with Splunk data effortlessly. - Discover Knowledge Objects Identify and access relevant saved searches, lookups, and other knowledge assets. - Execute Searches Run powerful Splunk queries to extract insights and drive intelligent workflows. - Leverage AI capabilities from Splunk’s AI Assistant for SPL & MLTK SPL search generation from natural language, search optimization, search explanation, retrieve MLTK models and algorithms.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Splunk DBX Add-on for Snowflake JDBC - v1.2.4 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Description:
JDBC driver for Snowflake provides Snowflake JDBC driver. Drivers can be use by others Splunk apps like DB Connect.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: ThreatMon Incident Management - v1.0.3 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

  1. Takedown request flow fixed — findings table now shows all findings dynamically and takedown button works reliably.
  2. Set Status updates now instantly reflected in the incident list via KV Store.
  3. Incident table visual improvements.


Description:
The Incidenet Integration app is designed to simplify and automate the process of connecting Splunk with external incident management platforms. Many organizations struggle with fragmented monitoring and manual workflows when dealing with security alerts or operational issues. This app helps bridge that gap by enabling seamless incident synchronization, and tracking directly from Splunk. By reducing manual effort and ensuring faster response times, it empowers teams to act on critical events more efficiently and with greater confidence.
item.dc:creator: ThreatMon IT

item.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows - v10.0.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

  • Removed python3.7 support
  • Removed duplicate EVAL-reason_id from [source::XmlWinEventLog:Security]
  • New mapping for field user in Event Id 5140


Description:
*** Important: Read upgrade instructions and test add-on update before deploying to production *** The Splunk Add-on for Windows 5.0.0 introduced breaking changes. If you are upgrading from a version of the Splunk Add-on for Windows that is earlier than 5.0.0, you must follow the documented upgrade instructions to avoid data loss. A best practice is to test the upgraded version in a non-production environment before deploying to production. Neither the Splunk Add-on for Windows DNS version 1.0.1 nor the Splunk Add-on for Windows Active Directory version 1.0.0 is supported when installed alongside the Splunk Add-on for Windows version 6.0.0. The Splunk Add-on for Windows version 6.0.0 includes the Splunk Add-on for Windows DNS and the Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory. The Splunk for Microsoft Windows add-on includes predefined inputs to collect data from Windows systems and maps to normalize the data to the Common Information Model.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

item.title: Cato Networks Security App for Splunk - v1.0.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Release Notes - Cato Networks Security App for Splunk v1.0.0 (EA)
Initial Release
Release Date: March 2025
Version: 1.0.0

First production release of the Cato Networks Security App for Splunk, providing comprehensive security monitoring and threat analysis for the Cato SASE platform.


Short Description:
Splunk application providing dashboards and analytics for Cato Networks security activity and investigations.


Description:
Cato Networks provides a cloud-native, single-vendor SASE platform that converges networking and security in a global cloud service. The Cato Networks Security App for Splunk provides dashboards, searches, and visualizations for monitoring and investigating activity across the Cato platform. This application helps security and operations teams analyze network security events, investigate threats, and monitor activity across their Cato deployment. Current content includes a threats dashboard that provides an overview of security threats and related activity across the Cato platform. The dashboard presents aggregated insights such as threat volumes, timelines, top affected entities (for example hosts, users, or domains), and other high-level security indicators.
item.dc:creator: Cato Networks

item.title: VMware Carbon Black Cloud - v2.2.6 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

VMware Carbon Black Cloud App for Splunk v2.2.6

Splunk 10 Compatibility

  • Set python.version = python3.9 across all modular inputs, REST endpoints, alert actions, and custom commands for full Splunk 10.x Python 3.9 runtime support
  • Minimum supported Splunk version is now 9.3 (9.2 and older no longer supported)

CBC SDK Update

  • Upgraded vendored CBC SDK from 1.5.9 to 1.5.10
  • Removed dependency on backports-datetime-fromisoformat C extension, which caused ModuleNotFoundError on Splunk 10.2's Python 3.9.25 runtime
  • Removed dead backports and rpds vendor directories containing non-portable compiled artifacts

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed proxy SSL configuration in alert actions and custom commands — is identity comparisons were silently preventing HTTPS proxy connections from being established
  • Added export = system to metadata/default.meta for datamodels, tags, transforms, props, eventtypes, views, savedsearches, and commands — resolves issue where the VMWare_CBC datamodel was not visible to Splunk's search subsystem
  • Removed leftover props.conf.orig file

Security Updates

  • Updated requests to 2.32.4 (CVE-2024-47081)
  • Updated urllib3 to 1.26.19 (CVE-2024-37891)
  • Updated certifi to 2024.7.4 (CVE-2024-39689)
  • Updated validators to 0.21.0 (CVE-2023-45813)


Description:
The VMware Carbon Black Cloud App for Splunk is a single application to integrate your endpoint and workload security features and telemetry directly into Splunk dashboards, workflows and alert streams. This application connects with any Carbon Black Cloud offering and replaces the existing product-specific Carbon Black apps for Splunk. This app provides a unified solution to integrate Carbon Black Cloud Endpoint and Workload offerings with Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Cloud, and Splunk Enterprise Security (ES). Out-of-the-box, this app provides holistic visibility into the state of your endpoints and workloads through customizable dashboards and alert feeds in Splunk. For distributed Splunk environments, please use the Input Add-on (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5333/) and the Technology Add-on (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5334/).
item.dc:creator: Carbon Black Developer Network

item.title: Zscaler Technical Add-On for Splunk - v4.1.4 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

V4.1.4
Updated sourcetype configurations for 'zscalernss-web' and 'zscalernss-tunnel' sourcetypes to accommodate Web and Tunnel logs in the "Splunk CIM" and JSON formats.
Updated API URI content for audit logs fetched via the API to use the status ID captured in the response from the initialization call.


Description:
The Zscaler Technical Add-On for Splunk takes events from Zscaler data sources and maps these to Splunk’s Common Information Model, this can be leveraged by Splunk Enterprise Security and and app leveraging the CIM Data Model, including the Zscaler App for Splunk This TA should be installed as per Splunk's guidelines on TA installation, e.g. http://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/ES/5.0.0/Install/InstallTechnologyAdd-ons Full App and TA Documentarian is available here --> https://help.zscaler.com/downloads/zscaler-technology-partners/operations/zscaler-and-splunk-deployment-guide/Zscaler-Splunk-Deployment-Guide-FINAL.pdf
item.dc:creator: Zscaler

item.title: Zscaler Splunk App - v3.0.22 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

V3.0.22
Updated search efficiency across all dashboards.
V3.0.21
Updated ZScaler Overview dashboard adding a time selector
Updated Administrative Audit Dashboard updating to use a base search to power all the panels on the dashboard and fixing issues where only one of the audit sourcetypes was appearing in the panels
V3.0.20

Updated dashboard Zscaler Overview panel Recent Admin Activity. Removed Interface=UI so activity from all interface values show on the dashboard panel. Added Interface to the final table and removed duplicated Time field


Description:
The Zscaler App for Splunk provides detailed dashboards and reporting for all Zscaler products using Zscaler Nanolog Streaming and Log Streaming services. The Zscaler App for Splunk can also ingest DLP incident information, bringing full context for DLP incidents directly into Splunk Pre-built reports exist for Zscaler’s Internet Access and Private Access Platforms, this include: * Overview Dashboards showing highlights for your Zscaler products * Focussed dashboards for: - Threat Intelligence - Web Usage - Remote Access usage - Zscaler DLP - event and incident reports Zscaler is excited to deliver this tight integration with Splunk and will continue to enhance reports, dashboards and integrations for the Zscaler Splunk App. Please note this app has a key dependancy on the Zscaler TA for Splunk --> https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3865/ Full App and TA Documentarian is available here --> https://help.zscaler.com/downloads/zscaler-technology-partners/operations/zscaler-and-splunk-deployment-guide/Zscaler-Splunk-Deployment-Guide-FINAL.pdf
item.dc:creator: Zscaler

item.title: Cloudflare App for Splunk - v2.3.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

(1) Compliant with Splunk v10
(2) Update the timestamp extraction so it uses EdgeStartTimestamp when present and falls back to Datetime for Firewall events


Description:
The Cloudflare App for Splunk makes analyzing Cloudflare logs easy and provides you with insights on the performance, reliability, and security of your websites and applications on the Cloudflare network. Use these insights to tune your Cloudflare configuration and provide the best experience for your end users. ## The application includes seventeen (17) dashboards powered by a custom Cloudflare Data Models. ##The application also includes five (5) dashboards for ZeroTrust logs based on index searches. ##This application also includes one (1) dashboard for Area1 logs based on index searches. ## By default, the application is shipped with Data Model Acceleration turned off but can be optionally turned on for improved speeds when loading dashboards and panels. ## The application also includes Common Information Model (CIM) compliant search-time field extractions and field aliases. Furthermore, line breaking configurations are included in the application. The Cloudflare App for Splunk is compatible with all Splunk Enterprise 9.x - 7.x releases and supports single Search Head deployments as well as being deployed on Search Head Clusters. ## Dashboards are broken up into 5 categories: Snapshot, Performance, Reliability, Security, and Zero Trust. Snapshot: - Cloudflare – Snapshot (Web Traffic Overview) - Cloudflare – Snapshot (Web Traffic Types) Performance: - Cloudflare – Performance (Hostname, Content Type, Request Method, Connection Type) - Cloudflare – Performance (Requests and Cache) - Cloudflare – Performance (Bandwidth) - Cloudflare – Performance (Static vs. Dynamic Content) Reliability: - Cloudflare – Reliability (Summary) - Cloudflare – Reliability (Detailed) Security: - Cloudflare – Security (Overview) - Cloudflare – Security (Bots Summary) - Cloudflare – Security (Bots Detailed) - Cloudflare – Security (Rate Limit) - Cloudflare – Security (WAF) - Cloudflare – Security Events ZeroTrust: - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - Access - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - CASB - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - Gateway - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - Device Posture - Cloudflare - Zero Trust - Network Sessions - Cloudlfare - Area1
item.dc:creator: Cloudflare, Inc

item.title: Censys ASM Add-on for Splunk Platform - v1.3.1 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Adding proxy parameter to the censysasmhosts command to allow a proxy to be used for that command.


Description:
The Censys Add-on for Splunk allows Censys ASM users to import Logbook data into Splunk®, where changes in their attack surface can be easily directed to downstream security and analytics applications.
item.dc:creator: Censys Inc

item.title: Supporting Add-on for Expressway CDR - v0.5.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

First public release


Short Description:
Specifies index-time settings to correctly ingest syslog-formatted CDR records from Cisco Expressway.


Description:
This Supporting Add-on defines index-time settings to ingest syslog-formatted CDR records from Cisco Expressway into Splunk. It is to be deployed on your Splunk Indexers (or if you are using a Heavy Forwarder and forwarding cooked data to your indexes, installed on that HF). It is most effective when also deployed with two other apps on the Splunk Search Head(s) or Search Head Cluster: a) Sideview's Supporting App for Cisco Expressway, and b) Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" app. With these pieces in place the Sideview apps on the search head implement a general purpose investigation and reporting solution for this data. NOTE: There are also some references in the app's config to other sourcetypes cisco_expressway_cdr_csv and/or cisco_expressway_cdr_json. These are still under development.
item.dc:creator: Sideview, LLC - Partner

item.title: Supporting App for Expressway CDR - v0.5.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

First public release


Short Description:
This app implements various field extraction configuration and Sideview conventions so as to allow syslog-formatted Expressway CDR data to be not only searched in Splunk effectively, but also to be pulled into Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" solution on Splunk and investigated and analyzed there.


Description:
This app implements various field extraction configuration and Sideview conventions so as to allow syslog-formatted Expressway CDR data to be not only searched in Splunk effectively, but also to be pulled into Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" solution on Splunk and investigated and analyzed there. NOTE that this Supporting App is designed to be always deployed with Sideview's "Supporting Add-on for Expressway CDR" ALSO deployed on the Indexing tier. That "Add-on" component contains crucial index-time configuration to index the CDR into Splunk correctly, and that index-time configuration is NOT duplicated in this app here. Therefore if you intend to set up a standalone deployment you will need both the TA and the SA. These two apps are furthermore designed to work with Sideview's commercial "Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics" app. When all three components are present and deployed to the right tiers, users can navigate, troubleshoot and build ad-hoc charts and reports around the Expressway data, within the the Cisco CDR app's complex user interfaces.
item.dc:creator: Sideview, LLC - Partner

item.title: Druv Prism - v1.2.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

v1.2.0 — Initial Splunkbase Release

What's new

  • Launcher dashboard for Druv Prism SaaS platform
  • Natural language to SPL query assistant accessible directly from Splunk Cloud
  • One-click launch to prism.druv.tech from within your Splunk environment

Requirements

  • Splunk Cloud
  • A Druv Prism account — visit prism.druv.tech to get started

Notes

This is the initial public release of the Druv Prism Splunk app.
Splunk Enterprise support is on the roadmap — contact sales@druv.tech to register interest.


Short Description:
AI-powered natural language query assistant for Splunk Cloud. This app is a launcher for the Druv Prism SaaS platform — install it, click Launch Druv Prism, and start querying your Splunk data in plain English. Zero SPL required. A Druv Prism account is required.


Description:
Druv Prism removes the SPL bottleneck for your entire Splunk organization. Type a question in plain English — Druv Prism translates it into a governed, optimized SPL query, executes it against your Splunk Cloud instance, and returns results with auto-generated visualizations. Zero SPL required. 10x faster time-to-insight. 80% reduction in analyst wait time for ad-hoc data requests. Key capabilities: - Natural Language to SPL — no query language expertise needed. Any analyst, compliance officer, or executive can query Splunk without writing a single line of SPL. - Schema Intelligence — automatically discovers your indexes, sourcetypes, and field mappings for precise, executable queries. No hallucinated field names. - Query Governance — every AI-generated query passes through a mandatory governance gate enforcing role-based access controls, time bounds, and scan budgets before execution. - Continuous Learning — improves with per-tenant feedback and few-shot examples. Gets smarter the more your team uses it. - Auto-Visualizations — results rendered automatically as charts, time series, and tables. No manual configuration required. Compatible with Splunk Cloud. This add-on serves as a launcher for the Druv Prism SaaS platform. After installation, click Launch Druv Prism to access the full natural language analytics experience at prism.druv.tech. Start a free 15-day trial at prism.druv.tech or contact sales@druv.tech to discuss enterprise plans.
item.dc:creator: Udaya Dasari

item.title: Nexthink connector TA for Splunk - v1.0.0 [New App Release]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

1.0.0 (Initial Release)

Features

  • NQL API v2 Data Collection — Collect endpoint data from Nexthink by executing saved NQL queries via OAuth2 client credentials authentication
  • Multi-Region Support — Connect to Nexthink cloud instances across US, EU, Asia Pacific (PAC), and Middle East (META) regions
  • Customizable Sourcetypes — Each input generates events with a configurable sourcetype in the format nexthink:{suffix} for flexible data organization
  • Built-in CIM Mapper — Visual interface for mapping Nexthink fields to Splunk Common Information Model fields at search time using FIELDALIAS definitions, with automatic eventtype and tag generation
  • Monitoring Dashboard — Track data ingestion health, error rates, and resource consumption
  • Resilient Collection — Automatic retry with exponential backoff for transient API errors (HTTP 429, 5xx, timeouts, connection failures)
  • Encrypted Credential Storage — OAuth2 Client Secrets are stored securely using Splunk's built-in credential management
  • Search Head Cluster Support — Custom configuration files are automatically replicated across SHC members

Supported Data Sources

  • Any data accessible via Nexthink's saved NQL queries, including device inventory, software installations, digital experience scores, alerts, user activity, and compliance data


Short Description:
The Nexthink Add-on for Splunk enables organizations to ingest endpoint analytics data from Nexthink directly into Splunk.


Description:
The Nexthink Add-on for Splunk enables organizations to ingest endpoint analytics data from Nexthink directly into Splunk by executing saved NQL (Nexthink Query Language) queries via the NQL API v2. Key Capabilities Connects to Nexthink's cloud platform using OAuth2 client credentials authentication Executes saved NQL queries on a configurable collection interval Ingests results as structured JSON events with customizable sourcetypes (nexthink:*) Supports multiple Nexthink accounts and regions (US, EU, Asia Pacific, Middle East) Includes a built-in CIM Mapper UI for mapping Nexthink fields to Splunk Common Information Model fields at search time via FIELDALIAS definitions Automatically generates eventtype and tag configurations for CIM compliance Provides a monitoring dashboard for tracking data ingestion health and errors Implements retry logic with exponential backoff for resilient data collection Use Cases IT Operations: Monitor device health, software inventory, and endpoint compliance across your environment Digital Experience: Bring Nexthink's digital experience scores and metrics into Splunk for correlation with other IT data sources Security Operations: Enrich security investigations with endpoint context from Nexthink, including device details, OS versions, and user activity Asset Management: Maintain an up-to-date inventory of devices, hardware configurations, and software installations within Splunk Supported Nexthink Regions US (United States) EU (Europe) PAC (Asia Pacific) META (Middle East) Data Flow The add-on authenticates with Nexthink's OAuth2 endpoint, executes your saved NQL queries, and writes each result record as an individual JSON event into the Splunk index of your choice. CIM field mappings are applied at search time through FIELDALIAS definitions managed by the built-in CIM Mapper, requiring no data transformation at ingest time.
item.dc:creator: Intelli Platforms

item.title: Infigo SIEM Content - v4.2.120260324131314 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

savedsearches.conf
- Modified rule_id: END010 — Endpoint - Windows - Suspicious Certutil Command Usage: you see drilldown_federated and drilldown_search logic updated; introduced_in moved to 4.2.0; rule_version updated to 1.0.2.


Description:
This application is a part of the Infigo SIEM package: https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7147 Application provides default security rules with Infigo SIEM. There are 135 rules available out of the box. Visit the official Infigo SIEM site for additional information about the product. Official site: www.infigosiem.com
item.dc:creator: Infigo IS

item.title: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow - v10.0.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Depreciated the support for python3.7 version
Security fixes


Description:
The Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow allows a Splunk software administrator to collect data from ServiceNow and create incidents and events in ServiceNow. The add-on collects incident, event, change, user, user group, location, and CMDB CI information from ServiceNow via ServiceNow REST APIs. The add-on also provides workflow actions that allow users to link directly from events in the Splunk platform search results to relevant ServiceNow incidents, events, and Knowledge Base articles. The Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow allows Splunk software administrators to use custom commands, alert actions, and scripts to create new incidents and events in your ServiceNow instance, as well as update the incidents created from the Splunk platform. This add-on provides the inputs and CIM-compatible knowledge to use with other Splunk apps, such as Splunk Enterprise Security and the Splunk App for PCI Compliance.
item.dc:creator: Splunk LLC

To avoid data loss or data duplication, follow the documented upgrade instructions in detail. If your are upgrading an earlier version of the Splunk Add-on for Salesforce, a best practice is to test your update in a non-production environment before deploying to production. The Splunk Add-on for Salesforce allows a Splunk software administrator to collect different types of data from Salesforce using REST APIs. The data includes: * Event log file data, https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/using_resources_event_log_files.htm. * Output of Salesforce object queries (SOQL). This add-on provides the inputs and CIM-compatible knowledge to use with other Splunk apps, such as Splunk Enterprise Security, the Splunk App for PCI Compliance, and Splunk IT Service Intelligence.

We will send notifications for instance when publishing new releases, so we can you let you know the cool and powerful things we've been working on! https://trackme-solutions.com/subscribe/ TrackMe has transitioned to a new licensing model. The Free Community Edition has been discontinued. What this means for you: - Existing licensed customers are not impacted. Your current license continues unchanged. - Community users are automatically upgraded to a Foundation Edition trial for 90 days, with full functionality enabled. After the trial period: - TrackMe remains active and fully usable for monitoring - The platform enters read-only mode You can continue to view data, dashboards, alerts, and history Creation of new tenants, trackers, or entities will be disabled To continue using TrackMe with full capabilities after the trial, a Foundation license is required. Buy Foundation Edition: https://trackme-solutions.com/get-foundation/ See all plans: https://trackme-solutions.com/pricing/

item.title: Owl Diode Receiver Add-On - v1.2.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Splunk recommended updates
Splunk Enterprise Compatibility
Splunk Web Compatibility


Description:
This Add-On works together with the Owl Diode Sender Add-On (https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5554/). It is designed to send data between Splunk servers that can pass through a data diode, while preserving the Splunk metadata. The sender will encapsulate Splunk metadata like sourcetype, source, host, _time into the _raw message which can then be forwarded over syslog via UDP or TCP. The receiver will unpack this and populate the metadata fields as well and restore the _raw to its original state.
item.dc:creator: Owl Product Management

It is designed to send data between Splunk servers that can pass through a Owl Data Diode, while preserving the Splunk metadata. The sender will encapsulate Splunk metadata like sourcetype, source, host, _time into the _raw message which can then be forwarded over UDP or TCP. The receiver will unpack this and populate the metadata fields as well and restore the _raw to its original state.

Infigo SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) not only has all the great features that you expect from a modern SIEM but also has advanced capabilities like multitenancy and dynamic assets discovery right out of the box. Infigo IS has been strictly in the information security business since 2005, doing offensive and defensive side, consulting, building apps on Splunk since 2012 – Infigo SIEM is the result of our combined experience, born from frustration of using products that underdelivered on their (security and compliance) promises. This is the fourth iteration of our product, the same product that we've integrated in telecoms, financial institutions, air traffic controls, across myriad industries, often in mission-critical environments. We can't afford to build a bad product, not just because of our customers, but because we use Infigo SIEM as the heart of our managed security services. item.title: TheHive/Cortex - v4.1.0 [Version Update]
item.description:
Platform: Splunk


Release Notes:

Release Notes

Summary

Version 4.1.0 introduces significant enhancements to user interaction and data enrichment capabilities between Splunk and TheHive/Cortex. This release focuses on investigation workflows, periodic data collection, visual identity, and operational robustness.

Deployment Notes

  • Prerequisite: Python 3.9+ (as per UCC standard).
  • Migration Path:
    • From v4.0.0: This is a non-breaking update. Existing configuration is preserved.
    • From v3.9.0 (Direct Upgrade): You can upgrade directly from v3.9.0 to v4.1.0 without installing v4.0.0 first.
    • ⚠️ CRITICAL: If upgrading from v3.x, the v4.0.0 migration instructions are mandatory and remain valid for this version (UCC architecture migration, breaking configuration changes, and new credential storage). Please read them carefully before proceeding.

New Features

📅 New Modular Inputs: Tasks, Timeline & Status

Three new data collection mechanisms have been added to track your cases and platform health:

  • TheHive Tasks Collection: Periodically retrieve all tasks associated with cases for better operational monitoring.
  • TheHive Timeline Collection: Automatically fetch case timeline events to build a complete history of investigations in Splunk.
  • TheHive Instance Status: Monitor your TheHive instances' health, version, and license status directly from Splunk.
  • Backfill Support: Dedicated backfill scripts are available for Tasks and Timeline to recover historical data.

🛡️ Add Timeline Event Alert Action

A new Custom Alert Action (and Adaptive Response) "TheHive - Add timeline event" is now available.

  • Analysts can now automatically or manually push findings directly to a case's timeline.
  • Supports custom titles (with <inheritance> from search name), descriptions, and specific start/end dates.
  • Fully integrated into the Audit Logs dashboard for tracking.

🔢 Automatic Case Number Resolution

The Add-on now handles both internal IDs (~12345) and human-readable case numbers (2392).

  • If a case number is provided in any alert action (Add Observables, Add Timeline Event), the app automatically resolves it to the correct internal ID via the API.
  • This simplifies workflows for analysts who primarily work with case numbers in the UI.

🎨 High-Quality Visual Identity

All Custom Alert Actions have been updated with unique, high-resolution hexagonal icons.

  • TheHive Actions: Official Yellow (#FFC107) branding.
  • Cortex Actions: Official Light Blue (#87CEFA) branding.
  • Optimized with transparent backgrounds for seamless integration into Splunk's Dark and Light themes.

Improvements & Bug Fixes

⏱️ Standardized Polling Intervals

  • Implemented dynamic interval flooring across all modular inputs (thehive_tasks, thehive_timeline, thehive_alerts_cases, etc.).
  • All collection time ranges are now aligned to clean boundaries (e.g., 12:51:00 to 12:52:00), eliminating "seconds drift" in logs and search results.

📊 Enhanced Audit Logs Dashboard

The Audit Logs dashboard has been significantly updated to provide better operational visibility:

  • Comprehensive Action Tracking: Now includes dedicated filters and metrics for all Custom Alert Actions, including the new Timeline Event action.
  • Data Collection Monitoring: The dashboard now tracks the execution and results of the new modular inputs (Tasks, Timeline, and Status), ensuring full traceability of your data ingestion.
  • Improved Success Monitoring: Logic updated to track specific event codes (e.g., TH201 for successful timeline events, CAA-THCA-125 for cases), allowing for clear distinction between successful creations and operational issues.

🔧 Indexing & Props

  • Fixed thehive:timeline sourcetype to ensure Splunk prioritizes normalized _time from JSON payloads.
  • Cleaned up build process to automatically remove Windows binaries (.exe, .pyd), Python compiled files (.pyc), and caches for Splunk Cloud compatibility.


Description:
This TA allows to add interaction features between TheHive or Cortex (https://www.strangebee.com/) and Splunk. It allows to retrieve all kind of information from TheHive/Cortex and to perform actions on these instances using Splunk, from a search or from a predefined dashboard. This TA is supporting only TheHive 5. For having an app supporting TheHive 3 or 4, please check the oldest releases. It's also supporting the Cortex 3 version. More information here: https://github.com/LetMeR00t/TA-thehive-cortex
item.dc:creator: Alexandre Demeyer

Deployed on the Akamai Intelligent Edge Platform, Akamai MFA can be activated and managed centrally via Enterprise Center with global reach and scale for resilience and performance. Description:
VulDB is the number one vulnerability database documenting and explaining security vulnerabilities and exploits since 1970. The VulDB app for Splunk integrates vulnerability data from VulnDB into Splunk to enhance vulnerability management (VM) and cyber threat intelligence (CTI). Defending against exploits, maintaining patch and release management becomes much easier with our unique Threat Intelligence Platform (TIP). You need to link the Splunk app to your VulDB account to use the API credits to fetch the infosec data. The amount of available API credits is defined by the license of your account (free, commercial or enterprise). You are able to purchase more API credits at https://vuldb.com/?pay We provide interoperability with information security data and cybersecurity products by MITRE and NVD (CVE, CWE, CPE, ATT&CK), Tenable Nessus, Qualys Scanner, Rapid7 MetaSploit MSF, Cisco, Microsoft, Palo Alto, Fortinet, Symantec, Trend Micro, FireEye, McAfee, ISS, IBM X-Force, Snyk, and other vendors.
